title: Metabolically competent cells, methods of making, and uses thereof
abstract: Provided herein are genetically engineered cells containing one or more modulated metabolic genes, where the expression of the modulated metabolic gene(s) can be greater than that of an unmodified control. Also provided herein are methods of making the genetically engineered cells using synergistic activation mediator CRISPR-Cas9. Further provided herein are high throughput assays that can employ the genetically engineered cells provided herein.
claims: Claims We claim: 1 . A genetically engineered cell comprising: one or more modulated metabolic genes, wherein the expression of the one or more modulated metabolic genes is greater than the expression in an unmodified control cell. 2. The genetically engineered cell of claim 1 , wherein the genetically engineered cell is an immortalized cell. 3. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein the one or more modulated genes is a cytochrome P450, phase I , or phase I I gene. 4. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein the genetically engineered cell is metabolically competent. 5. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein the genetically engineered cell comprises one or more sgRNAs for one or more metabolic genes. 6. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein the genetically engineered cell comprises a Cas9. 7. The genetically engineered cell of claim 6, wherein the Cas9 is inactive. 8. The genetically engineered cell of claim 6, wherein the Cas9 is operatively linked to a VP64 transcriptional activator. 9. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 or 6-7, wherein the genetically engineered cell comprises one or more Cas9 transcriptional activators. 10. The genetically engineered cell of claim 9, wherein the one or more CAs9 transcriptional activators can be a VP64 transcriptional activator, a MS2-p65-HSF 1 co- activation complex, a MS2 RNA aptamer sgRNA, and combinations thereof. 1 1 . The genetically engineered cell of claim 9, wherein the one or more Cas9 transcriptional activators form a synergistic activation mediator. 12. The genetically engineered cell of any one of claims 1 , wherein the one or more modulated metabolic genes are selected from the group consisting of: the genes set forth in Table 1 and any combination thereof. 13. A method of producing a genetically engineered cell of claim 1 , the method comprising: enhancing transcription of one or more metabolic genes in a cell using synergistic activation mediator CRISPR-Cas9, wherein transcription of one or more metabolic genes is increased as compared to an unmodified control cell. 14. A screening assay comprising the steps of: exposing a genetically engineered cell as in any of claims 1 - 12 with a test chemical or compound for a period of time; and measuring a physiologic characteristic of the genetically engineered cell. 15. The screening assay of claim 16, further comprising the step of determining the toxicity of the test chemical or compound from the measured physiologic characteristic.
